issues.detail: The requirement says “unfunded or pre-scoped projects have no assistant” and availability begins “from in_progress onward.” The test covers `open`, `matched_pending_fuel`, and an UNFUNDED pre-scoped fixture, but funding is allowed from `draft` onward; it never covers a funded-but-pre-scoped project and omits `scoped`/`triage` negatives.
issues.fix: Test every pre-`in_progress` lifecycle state, including a funded `draft` or `discovery_in_progress` project, and assert that the surface remains Discovery rather than the assistant.

issues.detail: The requirement promises “status, open blockers explained, recent progress summarized, fuel runway.” Requiring answers merely to “reflect the sentinels” can pass if the assistant echoes a task, blocker, fuel value, and activity event without explaining blockers, summarizing progress, or reporting runway.
issues.fix: Add explicit prompts and judgeable assertions for project/task status, an explanation of open blockers, a summary of recent progress, and fuel runway derived from controlled fixtures.

issues.detail: The test requires a completed project’s assistant to remain billable “not by lifecycle.” REQ-006/REQ-012 state that at completion leftover project fuel is released to general balance, project keys terminate, and the provider workspace is archived; the PRD also says post-completion new paid work uses a new project. No completed-project fuel pot, workspace, or top-up/metering path is defined.
issues.fix: Resolve the PRD decision first: either define how completed-project assistant usage is funded and metered after archival, or limit billable assistant availability. Then align this test with that decision.

issues.detail: “Given the funded transition … reframed from Discovery to assistant” treats funding itself as the reframe trigger. REQ-004 says a funded project can continue Discovery using fuel, while REQ-033 says the assistant is available only “from in_progress onward.” An early Discovery top-up must not activate the assistant.
issues.fix: Specify the `matched_pending_fuel` → `in_progress` kickoff transition as the reframe trigger, and separately retain Discovery for funded projects that remain before `in_progress`.

issues.detail: The coverage row claims “Reuses Discovery surface/model; no new chat infra; on-demand text Q&A,” but the test checks only “one model family.” One family does not prove reuse of the same model, and inspection alone does not exercise on-demand text Q&A.
issues.fix: Assert the exact Discovery model configuration is reused, send a text question and observe a text answer, and verify that no assistant response is initiated without an NGO request.

issues.detail: “No second chat infrastructure exists” has no defined observable identity or pass/fail probe; visual inspection of one surface cannot prove that a separate backend, conversation store, or request path was not introduced.
issues.fix: Name observable invariants such as the same frontend component, request handler/route, conversation identifier/store, and model configuration before and after the lifecycle transition.

issues.detail: The test asserts “no cap, no refusal threshold, no warning gate beyond the always-visible cost display.” The requirement only removes the scope guardrail and explicitly requires refusal at zero fuel; the Platform Promise also requires NGO-chosen project budgets and nothing charged beyond commitment.
issues.fix: Limit the assertion to absence of topic/scope-based refusal while positive fuel remains. Preserve fuel exhaustion, budget, safety/AUP, and operational controls.

issues.detail: The coverage map claims the test covers “No scope guardrail,” but sustained heavy usage tests quantity, not scope relevance. An implementation could reject every off-project question and still pass.
issues.fix: With positive fuel, submit clearly unrelated but otherwise allowed text requests and assert they are not declined, redirected, capped, or gated because they are outside project scope.
